name: Integrated Terminal
description: Embeds a real ConPTY-backed terminal within the application supporting multiple named tabs, full VT100 emulation, command history, working directory tracking, keyboard input handling (arrow keys, Enter, and other keypresses forwarded to the active process), and Ctrl+C interrupt.

ConPtyTerminal.cs.content:
class ConPtyTerminal : IDisposable
  L161: EXTENDED_STARTUPINFO_PRESENT: uint = 0x00080000
  L162: PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_PSEUDOCONSOLE: IntPtr = (IntPtr)0x00020016
  L163: STILL_ACTIVE: uint = 259
  L164: STARTF_USESTDHANDLES: int = 0x00000100
  HistoryIndex: int
  HasBeenUsed: bool
  L199: AddCommand(string command) -> void
  L209: GetCommandFromEnd(int historyIndex) -> string?
  WorkingDirectory: string
  ProcessId: int
  L348: SendLine(string text) -> void
  L353: SendRaw(string text) -> void
  L364: SendInterrupt() -> void
  L369: GetOutputText() -> string
  L374: ClearOutput() -> void
  L381: Dispose() -> void

Models/VtScreenBuffer.cs.content:
class VtScreenBuffer
  L19: MaxScrollbackLines: int = Constants.AppConstants.ScrollbackLimit
  L48: Process(string data) -> void
  L57: Render() -> string
  L89: Clear() -> void

Managers/TerminalTabManager.cs.content:
class TerminalTabManager : IDisposable
  L54: UpdateWorkingDirectory(string directory) -> void
  L125: AddTerminal(string? workingDirectory = null) -> void
  L224: CloseTerminal(int index) -> void
  L256: SwitchTo(int index) -> void
  L291: SendCommand() -> void
  L318: SendInterrupt() -> void
  L324: HandleKeyDown(KeyEventArgs e) -> void
  L402: HandleOutputKeyDown(KeyEventArgs e) -> void
  L437: HandleOutputTextInput(System.Windows.Input.TextCompositionEventArgs e) -> void
  L629: Dispose() -> void

patterns: VT100 screen buffer with scrollback limit
